metadata.career_relevance: Razorpay's analytics team answers millions of 'what was the total payment volume between timestamps A and B?' queries daily. Without prefix sums: O(n) per query = system unusable. With prefix sums: O(1) per query after O(n) precomputation. This pattern appears in every competitive coding round.

hook.real_world_problem:
```
Razorpay payment volume queries:
  daily[] = [100, 250, 180, 320, 150, 280]
  Q: 'Total volume from day 1 to day 4?'
  Naive: sum daily[1..4] = 250+180+320+150 = 900 → O(n) per query
  
  With 1M queries/day → 1M × n = unusable!

  Prefix sums: precompute in O(n), answer each query in O(1)
  prefix[] = [0, 100, 350, 530, 850, 1000, 1280]
  Query(1,4): prefix[5] - prefix[1] = 1000 - 100 = 900 ← O(1)!
```
hook.what_you_will_build: Prefix sum array for O(1) range queries, and subarray sum problems.

visual_aid.description: Prefix sum array diagram. Top: original array [3,1,2,5,8]. Bottom: prefix array [0,3,4,6,11,19]. Arrows show: prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + nums[i]. Range query: rangeSum(1,3) = prefix[4]-prefix[1] = 11-3=8 highlighted with bracket.
visual_aid.alt_text: Prefix sum array and range query

worked_example.scenario: Subarray Sum Equals K using prefix sums + HashMap.
worked_example.problem_statement: Count subarrays summing to k. nums=[1,1,1], k=2 → 2.

step_by_step_solution.details: prefix[j] - prefix[i] = k means subarray [i..j-1] sums to k. For each j, count how many i's have prefix[i] = prefix[j] - k. HashMap stores frequency of each prefix sum seen so far.

things_to_notice: prefix.put(0,1): handles subarrays starting at index 0
Works with negatives: sliding window fails for negative arrays

industry_examples.use_case: O(1) range sum queries after O(n) preprocessing. 1M daily queries answered in O(1M) instead of O(1M × n).

applications.interview_relevance: 'Range sum query' always → prefix sums. 'Subarray sum equals k' → prefix + HashMap. Two key patterns to memorize.
applications.real_world_workflow: Pattern: (1) precompute prefix[0..n] where prefix[0]=0, (2) rangeSum(l,r) = prefix[r+1]-prefix[l], (3) for count problems: HashMap tracks prefix frequencies.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: prefix has n+1 elements (index 0..n). prefix[0]=0. rangeSum(l,r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]. Students use prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] which breaks for l=0.
common_gaps.remediation: Always use n+1 sized prefix array with prefix[0]=0. rangeSum(l,r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]. The +1 shift handles all boundary cases.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Student uses prefix[r]-prefix[l] or prefix[r+1]-prefix[l+1] — off by one

key_takeaways: Subarray sum==k: HashMap counts prefix sums, check prefix[j]-k
Works with negatives: prefix sums don't care about sign
2D prefix: precompute rectangular sums in O(mn), query in O(1)

self_check_questions.question: Why initialize prefixCount.put(0,1) for subarray sum == k?
self_check_questions.answer: prefixCount.put(0,1) handles subarrays starting from index 0. If prefix[j] == k, then sum of nums[0..j-1] == k. We need to count this case, so we need prefix[i]=0 to have been seen once. That's why we initialize {0:1} — the 'empty prefix before the array starts'.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Dutch National Flag — partition array into 3 groups in O(n) O(1) space.
